problem_statement: Grouping and pivoting break on real-world data whenever a grouping key happens to be a string like 'constructor', '__proto__', 'toString', or another name of a built-in object member. Depending on the exact key, processing either throws a TypeError partway through or silently drops entire groups and columns from the result. We also noticed that groups keyed by numeric-looking strings such as '10' come back reordered instead of in first-seen order, which the documentation explicitly promises. Ordinary alphabetic keys behave fine. Please make grouping robust so any string key works and ordering guarantees hold.
notes: RE-LABELED 2026-07-06 from k=5 calibration (cheap=haiku 5/5, frontier=sonnet 5/5): no routing signal at authored difficulty — measured reality is cheap-solvable. authored_difficulty preserved.
